$description: BIM FLOOR PLATE STANDARDS token category — floor plate size hierarchy, tile fraction breakdown, main floor space allocations, and upper floor common area standards for the Key Plans and Tiles system. Source: AEC_Floor Plates_Key Plans and Tiles_V12.pdf (Collaborators #11, 2025-04-01); CONSTRUCTION_2026_01_06_Tear Sheet_Floor Plans_Key Plans_Methodology_FIN.xlsx (V3, 2025-11-29); AEC_Floor Plates_Key Plans_Notes.pdf (Collaborators #32, 2026-01-06). Area values are in m² (authoritative); SF equivalents in $description.

area.$description: 20,000 SF — net leasable area per upper floor plate. Basis for all tile fraction calculations. Does not include building core, egress stairs, or mechanical.

tile-fraction.$description: Standard tile fractions of the 20,000 SF net leasable floor plate. Tiles are self-similar and composable. Corporate Office tenants may occupy any fraction up to the full floor.

area.$description: 2,500 SF — 1/8 floor tile; minimum corporate office tile and Private Office leasehold aggregate tile.
5,000 SF — 1/4 floor tile; standard Professional Office leasehold tile and minimum mid-tier Corporate Office tile.
10,000 SF — 1/2 floor tile; Tenant Lounge occupies this fraction on the 2nd floor; large Corporate Office option.
15,000 SF — 3/4 floor tile; large Corporate Office option; not a standard Tile composition but a valid Corporate leasehold size.
20,000 SF — full floor tile; Corporate Office maximum; one tenant occupies entire upper floor.

second-floor.$description: Second floor is structured differently from upper floors — it carries the Tenant Lounge as the primary space.

area.$description: 10,000 SF — Tenant Lounge occupying the second floor (1/2 floor equivalent). Decorated staircase from main floor lobby connects to Tenant Lounge atrium per AEC_Sample Architects_Staircases_V12.pdf.

main-floor.$description: Main floor (ground level) contains building services, amenity, and circulation spaces rather than leasable office tiles.

area.$description: 900 SF — combined Building Manager office and Mail Room on main floor.
1,800 SF — Loading area and Recycling facility; service access on main floor.
1,650 SF — Coffee/Bread service amenity and Public Restrooms; main floor public amenity zone.

upper-floor-common.$description: Common area allocations on upper floors (above main floor and Tenant Lounge). These are non-leasable and reduce gross floor area to net leasable.

area.$description: 450 SF — bilateral corridor allocation per upper floor. Connects tenant leaseholds and provides egress path.
700 SF — Tenant Restrooms plus Meter Room allocation per upper floor.

furniture-availability.$description: Expected distribution of furniture configuration sizes across the tenant mix. Used for FFE procurement planning and Tile composition optimisation. Source: CONSTRUCTION_2026_01_06_Tear Sheet_Floor Plans_Key Plans_Methodology_FIN.xlsx.

small.$description: 80% — majority of tenant fit-outs use small furniture configurations. Drives Private Office and small Professional Office tile prevalence.

large.$description: 10% — large furniture configuration tenant mix; typically Corporate Office full-floor tenants.
